creator: gabrisgabor@gmail.com
text:
On my laptop, which is an Albacomp Eco Traveller V4 (as far as I know
it is a rebranded Clevo M660S), when I the built-in touchpad, the mouse
cursor randomly hangs for some seconds (approx. 4-5secs). It is likely
to happen serially, the cursor freezes for 5 seconds then it is released,
then hangs again and this can happen up to 4-5 times in series. Then I
can use the touchpad again normally, then it freezes again after some
time. During this, the following messages appear in dmesg:

can't re-use a leaf (directional_scrolls)!
can't re-use a leaf (low_speed_threshold)!
can't re-use a leaf (min_movement)!
can't re-use a leaf (squelch_level)!

dmesg in verbose mode gives:

battery0: battery initialization failed, giving up
kbdc: TEST_AUX_PORT status:0000
kbdc: RESET_AUX return code:00fa
kbdc: RESET_AUX status:00aa
kbdc: RESET_AUX ID:0000
can't re-use a leaf (directional_scrolls)!
can't re-use a leaf (low_speed_threshold)!
can't re-use a leaf (min_movement)!
can't re-use a leaf (squelch_level)!
kbdc: TEST_AUX_PORT status:0000
kbdc: RESET_AUX return code:00fa
kbdc: RESET_AUX status:00aa
kbdc: RESET_AUX ID:0000
can't re-use a leaf (directional_scrolls)!
can't re-use a leaf (low_speed_threshold)!
can't re-use a leaf (min_movement)!
can't re-use a leaf (squelch_level)!


I have hw.psm.synaptics_support=1 in /boot/loader.conf, but the problem persists without ths line.

A verbose dmesg of the machine:

Fix: 

Booting with ACPI disabled.
How-To-Repeat: It seems that it is in connection with battery status querying. 
It is more likely to happen without battery (on AC power) than with on
battery power.
